rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance
Ritual / practice / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no 'fixed' retrospectives
Register match — Godin × Sutherland × Siilasmaa (Bootstrap voice trio)
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 5: peer-warmth ('A chat forgets'); line 13: Sutherland-style reframe ('sabotage by shovelling'); line 195: action-oriented ('Each cycle smarter than the last')

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 110: 'compounding' used in body without earning—appears in maintainer frameworks list but not operationally taught in body. Also line 139: 'compounding' unnamed principle introduced before module context.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Either earn 'compounding' with one-breath definition at first use in body, or remove the abstract noun and describe the mechanic concretely.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + strategy docs, NOT student-facing body
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 139: 'A library without a librarian is a cost' teaches mechanics (agents reading memory), not positioning against vendors or consultancy

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 107: aspirational ('put it to work'); line 138: positive frame ('Quietly, you just made a reusable capability')

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 9: 'session' and 'task' appear in span tags without definition in body. Line 139-140: 'agent' and 'compounding' used as load-bearing concepts without earning.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Earn 'session' and 'task' distinction with one breath before or at line 9. Agent as a concept is operationally earned in Phase 2, so early span-tagged appearance is acceptable; compounding is not earned until closing (line 195), making line 110 and 139 premature.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Bootstrap default)
rules_evaluated.evidence: no unearned tech jargon in main body prose

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 5: 'A memory remembers' sets memory as persistent artifact. Line 9: memory vs session-context clearly separated by span tags

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in the body
rules_evaluated.evidence: no troubleshooting branches in main narrative; fallbacks appear only below maintainer cut

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust Claude's defaults for structure
rules_evaluated.evidence: prompts close with 'show me before saving' without over-specifying output shape

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview banned; compound shape varies
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 4 debrief uses audit-and-tier move, not repetitive retro shape

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
Section headers use command verbs when the section is about doing something
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 11: '**Phase 1. Curate, ingest, build.**' uses verb-form nouns but not imperative. Line 106: '**Phase 2. Your first custom agent.**' is a label, not an imperative.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Homogenize phase headers to pure imperatives or declarative labels consistently. Diagnostic: student is about to DO something; headers should lead with the verb or use consistent label pattern.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use the target's title, never its filename
rules_evaluated.evidence: no cross-module inline links in student-facing body

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 9: 'session' and 'task' appear in span tags without disambiguation. Line 110: 'agent' appears before Phase 2 teach.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add one-line disambiguation at first use: clarify 'session' (Claude Code runtime) vs 'memory' (persistent artifact); clarify 'agent' (Claude Code automation) vs 'your custom agent' (files you author).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 138: 'Claude reads the agent file, reads the memory' — should be 'the agent reads the memory' (autonomy framing). Line 142: 'Claude integrates' — ambiguous on autonomous vs invoked.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Use 'the agent' for autonomous work (reads memory, cites sources); use 'Claude' for addressee/UI reference ('Ask Claude to...'). Check for inverted agency in setup sentences.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose
Reflection beats stay invisible in the flow
rules_evaluated.evidence: no meta-pedagogy headers announcing reflection; prompts carry the work

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No meta-ask when the student has already decided
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 164-165: prompt asks student to judge whether claims are specific, then proposes rewrites—student hasn't pre-judged Claude's findings yet.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Reorder: Claude names the generic claim first, then student decides to accept/reject before asking Claude to rewrite.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: `.md` is the default file type for student artifacts
rules_evaluated.evidence: all prompt destinations use `.md` by default

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cut NVA: true but not moving the student forward
rules_evaluated.evidence: every detail in body drives toward the next action or artifact

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks
rules_evaluated.evidence: all prompts contain no [BRACKET] placeholders

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 74: context block before **Prompt** label, but no semantic lead-in. Line 113: missing lead-in entirely. Line 143: missing lead-in.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add one-sentence action lead-in BEFORE each **Prompt** label. Example: 'Ask Claude to read every source and build topic files.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block
rules_evaluated.evidence: all prompts follow pattern: context, **Prompt** *(Claude Code)*, blank line, fenced block

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path
rules_evaluated.evidence: no skills invoked in fenced blocks

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 45: 'curation plan we just agreed' — scrollback-only, no back-reference. Line 161: 'Pick 3 memory pages at random' — fuzzy, not deterministic.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add deterministic anchors: back-reference for Phase 2 ('the plan you approved'), explicit paths ('memory/topic-name.md'). For random selection, clarify scope ('in memory/ folder').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Question-dump default — `in turn` is too soft
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 152-155: Phase 4 audit prompt 'For each, is the top claim...' — no explicit one-at-a-time instruction.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add explicit: 'Ask about one page at a time. Wait for my answer before asking about the next.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Overwrite anxiety — name the overwrite default
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 77: 'create a markdown file' — good. But line 130-132: 'integrate it into the memory' — missing explicit overwrite instruction.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add 'overwrite existing files' or 'update the existing pages, don't create new versions' to Phase 3 and close prompts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Grill before save — collaborative quality at artefact assembly
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 92: agent file prompt missing 'grill me on...' question. Line 161: audit prompt missing grill before saving soft-pages list.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add grill step before save: Phase 2 agent — 'Before saving, ask me anything about the agent's rules that might trip up its first run.' Phase 4 audit — 'Before you save the list, ask me anything whose answer would sharpen these six proposals.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompt blocks require maintainer approval before edit
rules_evaluated.evidence: no edit history; prompt blocks have maintainer-reviewed tag below cut

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths (Bootstrap-specific)
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 77: 'create a markdown file in memory/' — missing module prefix. Line 92: 'Show me the file before saving' — agent file should be saved to explicit path like 'module-2/agents/'.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Specify full subpaths for Bootstrap: 'save to module-2/agents/<name>.md' for agent files, 'module-2/memory/<topic>.md' for memory pages.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named
rules_evaluated.evidence: exercise not a module; no Big Idea/Key Concepts/mood-contract section. Exercise mood is implicit: agency and ownership.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check
rules_evaluated.evidence: exercise is Module 2 anchor; strategy: 'students build their first memory around a real challenge'. This exercise delivers that.
